attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.effect: Does 30 damage times the number of your Pokémon that have the Round attack.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It glides on its outstretched membrane while shocking foes with the electricity stored in the pouches on its cheeks.

attacks.name: Outrage
attacks.effect: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Blue Flare
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: This legendary Pokémon can scorch the world with fire. It helps those who want to build a world of truth.

attacks.name: Synthesis
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Grass Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Revenge Blast
attacks.effect: Does 30 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Search your deck for 3 Pokémon Tool cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Echoed Voice
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Echoed Voice attack does 50 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Cinccino's body is coated in a special oil that helps it deflect attacks, such as punches.

attacks.name: Tail Slap
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Minccino greet each other by grooming one another thoroughly with their tails.

attacks.name: Relaxed Roll
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number heads.

flavorText: Using the feelers on its ears, it can tell how someone is feeling or when an egg might hatch.

attacks.name: Picnic Weather
attacks.effect: Put a Teddiursa from your discard pile onto your Bench. Then, attach an Energy card from your discard pile to that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: In its territory, it leaves scratches on trees that bear delicious berries or fruits.

attacks.name: Honey Snack
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It lets honey soak into its paws so it can lick them all the time. Every set of paws tastes unique.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Thanks to its unstable genetic makeup, this special Pokémon conceals many different possible evolutions.

attacks.name: Captivate
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Their cute act is a ruse. They trick people and steal their valuables just to see the looks on their faces.

attacks.name: Attract
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: When its opponent can't be paralyzed, it contorts itself with unexpected speed and flops away.

attacks.name: Brilliant Voice
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Round
attacks.effect: Does 30 damage times the number of your Pokémon that have the Round attack.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Eternal Radiance
attacks.effect: Move all damage counters from this Pokémon to the Defending Pokémon. This Pokémon can't use Eternal Radiance during your next turn.

flavorText: To protect its Trainer, it will expend all its psychic power to create a small black hole.

attacks.name: Tiptoe Step
attacks.effect: Draw a card for each Psychic Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Double Slap
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: If its Trainer becomes happy, it overflows with energy, dancing joyously while spinning about.

attacks.name: Hypnotic Gaze
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: If its horns capture the warm feelings of people or Pokémon, its body warms up slightly.

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It occasionally uses an electric shock to recharge a fellow Pikachu that is in a weakened state.

attacks.name: Charm
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: A poor walker, it often falls down. However, its strong pride makes it puff up its chest without a care.
A fire burns inside, so it feels very warm to hug. It launches fireballs of 1,800 degrees F.

attacks.name: Rest
attacks.effect: Heal all damage from this Pokémon. This Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Lunge
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Extremely loyal to its Trainer, it will bark at those who approach the Trainer unexpectedly and run them out of town.
It only gives its all against strong opponents who are not fazed by the glare from Serperior's noble eyes.

attacks.name: Leaf Blade
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: When it gets dirty, its leaves can't be used in photosynthesis, so it always keeps itself clean.

attacks.name: Growth
attacks.effect: Attach a Grass Energy from your hand to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Being exposed to sunlight makes its movements swifter. It uses vines more adeptly than its hands.

attacks.name: Outrage
attacks.effect: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Bolt Strike
attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 40 damage to itself.

flavorText: This Pokémon appears in legends. In its tail, it has a giant generator that creates electricity.

attacks.name: Outrage
attacks.effect: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.effect: Discard 2 Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon appears in legends. It sends flames into the air from its tail, burning up everything around it.

rules: Double Colorless Energy provides ColorlessColorless Energy.

Move a basic Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Put a Pokémon from your hand face down in front of you and tell your opponent its name. Your opponent guesses the height of that Pokémon. Reveal that Pokémon. If your opponent guessed right, he or she draws 3 cards. If your opponent guessed wrong, you draw 3 cards. Return the Pokémon to your hand. (You can't choose a Pokémon that doesn't have the height printed on the card.)

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Energy Wheel
attacks.effect: Move an Energy from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hurricane
attacks.effect: Move a basic Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The lower half of its body is wrapped in a cloud of energy. It zooms through the sky at 200 mph.

attacks.name: Revenge
attacks.effect: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack during his or her last turn, this attack does 70 more damage.

attacks.name: Head Charge
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: Their fluffy fur absorbs damage, even if they strike foes with a fierce headbutt.

attacks.name: Clutch
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It races through narrow caves, using its sharp claws to catch prey. The skin on its face is harder than a rock.

attacks.name: Echoed Voice
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Echoed Voice attack does 50 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Their white fur is coated in a special oil that makes it easy for them to deflect attacks.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: These Pokémon prefer a tidy habitat. They are always sweeping and dusting, using their tails as brooms.

attacks.name: Glare and Peck

flavorText: For some reason, it likes to land on people's heads softly and act like it's a hat.

attacks.name: Plasma Gale
attacks.effect: Discard a Plasma Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you can't discard a Plasma Energy, this attack does nothing.

attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dragonblast

flavorText: This brutal Pokémon travels the skies on its six wings. Anything that moves seems like a foe to it, triggering its attack.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: After it has eaten up all the food in its territory, it moves to another area. Its two heads do not get along.

attacks.name: Deep Growl
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Power Breath
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It tends to bite everything, and it is not a picky eater. Approaching it carelessly is dangerous.

attacks.name: Mach Cut
attacks.effect: Discard a Special Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dragonblade
attacks.effect: Discard the top 2 cards of your deck.

flavorText: It is said that when one runs at high speed, its wings create blades of wind that can fell nearby trees.

attacks.name: Shred
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It loves sparkly things. It seeks treasures in caves and hoards the loot in its nest.
It attacks using its huge mouth. While its attacks are powerful, it hurts itself out of clumsiness, too.

attacks.name: Dragon Pulse
attacks.effect: Discard the top 2 cards of your deck.

attacks.name: Shred
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It lives in the ozone layer far above the clouds and cannot be seen from the ground.

attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, discard all Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: On sunny days, it flies freely through the sky and blends into the clouds. It sings in a beautiful soprano.

attacks.name: Energy Press
attacks.effect: Does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Iron Breaker
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It has a body and heart of steel. Its glare is sufficient to make even an unruly Pokémon obey it.

attacks.name: Brutal Bash
attacks.effect: Does 20 damage times the number of Darkness Pokémon you have in play.

attacks.name: Dark Rush
attacks.effect: Does 20 damage times the number of damage counters on this Pokémon.

flavorText: Bonds between these Pokémon are very strong. It protects the safety of its pack by tricking its opponents.

attacks.name: Ascension
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon. (This counts as evolving this Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It changes into the forms of others to surprise them. Apparently, it often transforms into a silent child.

attacks.name: Night Spear
attacks.effect: Does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.effect: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a number of cards equal to the number of cards in your opponent's hand.

flavorText: Its constant mischief and misdeeds resulted in it being bound to an Odd Keystone by a mysterious spell.

attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from each of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Retaliate
attacks.effect: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack during his or her last turn, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: When trying to protect someone, it extends its elbows as if they were swords and fights savagely.

attacks.name: Aura Sphere
attacks.effect: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: A well-trained one can sense auras to identify and take in the feelings of creatures over half a mile away.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It has the peculiar power of being able to see emotions such as joy and rage in the form of waves.

attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Echoed Voice attack does 50 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

Since they have two divided brains, at times they suddenly try to take two different actions at once.

attacks.name: Nap
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Because their bodies are enveloped in a special liquid, they are fine in any environment, no matter how severe.
They drive away attackers by unleashing psychic power. They can use telepathy to talk with others

attacks.effect: Does 20 more damage for each Psychic Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Starry skies thousands of light-years away are visible in the space distorted by their intense psychic power.

attacks.name: Double Slap
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: They use hypnosis to control people and Pokémon. Tales of Gothorita leading people astray are told in every corner.

attacks.name: Trip Over
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It stares intently at everything. It can become so obsessed with watching that it doesn't notice attacks.

attacks.name: Hypnotic Gaze
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Double Slap
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Their ribbonlike feelers increase their psychic power. They are always staring at something.

attacks.name: Poison Gas
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Inhaling the gas they belch will make you sleep for a week. They prefer unsanitary places.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: Does 10 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: The guardians of an ancient city, they use their psychic power to attack enemies that invade their territory.

attacks.name: Energy Gift
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for 2 Psychic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It shakes its tail vigorously when it emits ultrasonic waves strong enough to reduce concrete to rubble.

attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The heart-shaped mark left on a body after a Woobat has been attached to it is said to bring good fortune.

flavorText: Its cry sounds like an incantation. It is said the cry may rarely be imbued with happiness-giving power.

attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

It picks food from cactus plants, deftly avoiding buds and spines. It seems to skip about to move.

flavorText: A legendary Pokémon that is said to live in thunderclouds. It freely controls lightning bolts.

attacks.effect: Does 20 damage times the number of your Pokémon that have the Round attack.

flavorText: It lives in the water and on land. It uses its long, sticky tongue to capture prey.
By vibrating its cheeks, it emits sound waves imperceptible to humans. It uses the rhythm of these sounds to talk.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.effect: Does 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Part of the armor on its anterior legs becomes a giant sword. Its cry alone is enough to intimidate most enemies.

attacks.name: Aqua Tail
attacks.effect: Flip a coin for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 10 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Scalchop techniques differ from one Dewott to another. It never neglects maintaining its scalchops.

attacks.name: Razor Shell
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: The scalchop on its stomach is made from the same elements as claws. It detaches the scalchop for use as a blade.

flavorText: It drifts in warm seas. It always returns to where it was born, no matter how far it may have drifted.

flavorText: If anyone were to hurt its pride, it would slash them with wings that can cleave through an ice floe.

attacks.name: Water Splash
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Because every Prinplup considers itself to be the most important, they can never form a group.

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: A legendary bird Pokémon. It can create blizzards by freezing moisture in the air.

attacks.name: Howling Rampage
attacks.effect: Does 20 damage times the number of Prize cards both players have taken.

flavorText: Once it begins to rampage, a Gyarados will burn everything down, even in a harsh storm.

attacks.name: Flailing Flop
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: A Magikarp living for many years can leap a mountain using Splash. The move remains useless, though.

attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 50 damage to itself.

attacks.effect: Does 10 or more for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon appears in legends. It sends flames into the air from its tail, burning up everything around it.
It can throw a fire punch by setting its fists on fire with its fiery chin. It cares deeply about its friends.

attacks.effect: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: When its internal fire flares up, its movements grow sharper and faster. When it trouble, it emits smoke.

attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It can deftly dodge its foe's attacks while shooting fireballs from its nose. It roasts berries before it eats them.

attacks.name: Turbo Energize
attacks.effect: Search your deck for 2 basic Energy cards and attach them to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Intensifying Burn
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, this attack does 50 more damage.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Stored Power
attacks.effect: Move all Energy attached to this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon brings victory. It is said that Trainers with Victini always win, regardless of the type of encounter.

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Fire Blast
attacks.effect: Discard a Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: One of the legendary bird Pokémon. It is said that its appearance indicates the coming of spring.

attacks.name: Color Coordination
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any basic Energy attached to it that is the same type as the Defending Pokémon, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: Each of its nine tails is imbued with supernatural power, and it can live for a thousand years.

attacks.name: Firebreathing
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: As each tail grows, its fur becomes more lustrous. When held, it feels slightly warm.

attacks.name: Split Bomb
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Scorching Fire
attacks.effect: Discard a Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is said that Charizard's fire burns hotter if it has experienced harsh battles.

attacks.name: Raging Claws
attacks.effect: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: In the rocky mountains where Charmeleon live, their fiery tails shine at night like stars.

attacks.name: Draw In
attacks.effect: Attach 2 Fire Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

flavorText: The fire on the tip of its tail is a measure of its life. If healthy, its tail burns intensely.

attacks.name: Gaia Blaster
attacks.effect: Does 20 more damage for each Grass Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon existed 300 million years ago. Team Plasma altered it and attached a cannon to its back.

attacks.name: Double Draw
attacks.effect: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Leaf Wallop
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Leaf Wallop does 40 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its head sprouts horns as sharp as blades. Using whirlwind-like movements, it confounds and swiftly cuts opponents.

attacks.name: Hard Press
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: When its boulder is broken in battles for territory, it feels unsure and begins to weaken.
When it finds a stone of a suitable size, it secretes a liquid from its mouth to open up a hole to crawl into.

attacks.name: Cutting Arm
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Upon finding a small Pokémon, it weaves clothing for it from leaves, using the cutters on its arms and sticky silk.

attacks.name: String Shot
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Forests where Swadloon live have superb foliage because the nutrients they make from fallen leaves nourish the plant life.
Since this Pokémon makes its own clothes out of leaves, it is a popular mascot for fashion designers.

attacks.name: Leaf Boomerang
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Leavanny dress it in clothes they made for it when it hatched. It hides its head in its hood while it is sleeping.

attacks.name: Leaf Tornado
attacks.effect: Move as many Grass Energy attached to your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: They raise their heads to intimidate opponents but only give it their all when fighting a powerful opponent.

attacks.name: Wring Out
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: They avoid attacks by sinking into the shadows of thick foliage. They retaliate with masterful whipping techniques.

attacks.name: Leaf Blade
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: They photosynthesize by bathing their tails in sunlight. When they are not feeling well, their tails droop.

attacks.name: Ambush Bite
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage and discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Using its tentacles to lash itself to trees, it lies in wait for prey, luring it close with sweet-smelling drool.

attacks.name: Double Spin
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The small ball is not only filled with nutrients, it is also tasty. Starly try to peck it off.

attacks.name: Share
attacks.effect: Heal 40 damage from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Double Stab
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The berries stored in its vaselike shell eventually become a thick, pulpy juice.

attacks.name: Bind Down
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Flog
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Even if one of its arms is eaten, it's fine. The Pokémon regenerates quickly and will go right back to normal.

attacks.name: Flog
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Many writhing vines cover it, so its true identity remains unknown. The blue vines grow its whole life long.
